metadata.description: Batches replacing generated fallback IPA with redistributable reviewed sources.
metadata.policy: Do not infer UK IPA from US-only sources. CMUdict token composites are US-only reviewed replacements for phrase entries.

batches.source_license: CMUdict permissive redistributable license; see upstream cmudict distribution
batches.selection_policy: First 500 generated-fallback US IPA entries whose headword tokens are all present in CMUdict, ordered by number and priority of project CORE_WORDS tokens, excluding proper nouns before common entries.

An international organization founded as a result of the Treaty of Versailles, which ended the First World War. Its goals included disarmament, preventing war through collective security, settling disputes between countries through negotiation, diplomacy and improving global quality of life. The predecessor of the United Nations.
Official name of Taiwan: a partly-recognized country in East Asia; the rump state left over from the Republic of China on the mainland after 1949.

Official name of Angola: a country in Central Africa.

A perennial herb, of species Melissa officinalis, of southern Europe; used as a flavouring in ice cream etc.

Either of two breeds of domestic duck used primarily for egg and meat production.
(psychoanalysis) The unconscious desire of a woman to possess a penis.

The valves controlling each pipe may be actuated electronically or using long wooden poles called trackers.
A card set down at a table to inform a guest (named on the card) where they are to sit.
(military) Any poisonous gas used as a chemical weapon.

A cocktail made from vodka, tomato juice, and usually other spices or flavorings, such as Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco sauce, beef consommé or bouillon, horseradish, celery or celery salt, salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and lemon juice.

Sugar which has been only partially refined and still contains molasses.

A major bus stop, one that serves as a transfer point between a large number of routes.

(UK, Ireland, ) Heated sugar spun into thin threads and collected into a mass, usually on a stick.

A sheet of paper (or a digital analogue thereof) containing notes used to assist (with or without permission) on a test; especially, such a sheet used secretly and improperly.
A flavoured preparation of chicle or synthetic rubber, made for chewing.
(carboxylic acid) A colourless crystalline compound, 2-hydroxy-1,2,3-propanetricarboxylic acid, C6H8O7, found in citrus fruit; it is used as a food additive and in the manufacture of citrates.
(British, Australia, NZ, Canada) An object (usually made of wood or plastic) used to attach wet laundry to a clothesline so that it (the laundry) can dry.
The organisation or officer enforcing maritime law and policing the seas within territorial waters.
A male pigeon.

A cylindrical section of a naturally occurring substance, typically obtained by drilling into the substance, for example sediment or rock, with a core drill.

A string of beads used by members of various religions to count prayers, chants, or devotions.

Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see prime,‎ factor.

A material in its unprocessed, natural state considered usable for manufacture.

The symbol §, used to refer to a particular section of a document, such as a legal code.
Any of several forms of vibrational waves that travel through the Earth as the result of an earthquake or underground explosion.

Too sweet; sickeningly sweet or sentimental.

A roller-skating rink; the building that houses such a surface.

Bread from a loaf that is sold sliced into pieces of equal thickness by an automated process.

Synonym of vending machine.

batches.selection_policy: Remaining generated-fallback US IPA entries whose headword tokens are all present in CMUdict, ordered by number and priority of project CORE_WORDS tokens, excluding proper nouns before common entries.

replacements.english: A place that is established to provide soup and other basic food as a charity or in time of disaster.

(dialectal, or, archaic) A song thrush.
A great ancient Greek historian (c. 460 BCE – c. 395 BCE) and author of the History of the Peloponnesian War, which recounts the 5th century BCE war between Sparta and Athens to the year 411 BCE.
A fairy tale about a thumb-sized girl.
(US, colloquial) An exclamation of surprise, agitation or petulance.
(meteorology) A large, dark cloud, usually a cumulonimbus, charged with electricity and producing thunder and lightning.
(by extension, figurative) Something menacing and brooding.

(anatomy, immunology) Ellipsis of thymus gland (“A ductless gland, consisting mainly of lymphatic tissue, located behind the top of the breastbone. It is most active during puberty, after which it shrinks in size. It plays an important role in the development of the immune system and produces lymphocytes.”).
A canton of Switzerland.
A left tributary of the Po in Switzerland and Italy.
The quality of being tidy.
A female tiger; a she-tiger.
A person who sets tiles.
A covering of tiles.
(informal) Aluminium foil.
A person who makes or repairs things with tin or similar alloys.
(slang, vulgar) A nipple.
Any of several European plants, of the genus Linaria, having two-lipped yellow flowers.
A city in Mie Prefecture, Japan.
(dated) A shop that sells tobacco products.
(music) A piece of music (usually for a keyboard instrument) designed to emphasise the dexterity of the performer.
Requiring continuous physical effort; laborious.
Three atolls forming a dependent territory of New Zealand in the South Pacific Ocean.
A city, the capital of the State of Mexico, in central Mexico.
A city, the administrative center of Tomsk Oblast, Russia.
(pathology) Inflammation of the tonsils.
(bullfighting) A bullfighter, especially one on horseback.
Made of the covering of carapace of hawksbill turtle.
The horny, translucent, mottled covering of the carapace of the hawksbill turtle, used as a veneer etc.
Any of several butterflies, mostly of the genera Nymphalis and Aglais that have similar markings.
A city in Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, and the préfecture of the département of Var, in France.
A path alongside a canal or river, originally for horses towing barges, now more often used as a footpath.
(medicine) An infectious disease of the eyelid caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis.
(geology) A pale igneous rock consisting mostly of potassium feldspar and plagioclase.
A skilled manual worker (implied male).

(architecture) The transversal part of a church, which crosses at right angles to the greatest length, and between the nave and choir. In the basilicas, this had often no projection at its two ends. In Gothic churches these project greatly, and should be called the arms of the transept. It is common, however, to speak of the arms themselves as the transepts.
Making a transit or passage.
(grammar, of a verb) Taking a direct object or objects.
(set theory, of a relation on a set) Having the property that if an element a is related to b and b is related to c, then a is necessarily related to c.
(algebra, of a group action) Such that, for any two elements of the acted-upon set, some group element maps the first to the second.
(physiology) The process of giving off water vapour through the skin or mucous membranes.
(mineral) A light, porous form of concretionary limestone (or calcite) deposited from solution, and sometimes quarried for building.
A foot-operated pedal or lever that generates motion.
Any of several plants of the pea family, having compound, trifoliate leaves; especially one of the genus Trifolium.
(musical instrument) A three-course stringed instrument similar to a guitar; the Cuban variant has six strings, and the Puerto Rican has nine.

(geometry, analysis) The branch of mathematics that deals with the relationships between the sides and angles of (in particular) right-angled triangles, as represented by the trigonometric functions, and with calculations based on said relationships.
(Hinduism) The trinity of supreme divinity, as represented by the forms of Brahma (the creator), Vishnu (the maintainer or preserver), and Shiva (the destroyer or transformer).
A stand with three short legs, especially for cooking over a fire.
(derogatory) A strumpet; a whore.
The territory ruled by a tsar.
(historical) A person who sets type; an employee in a printshop who manually selected pieces of movable type and assembled them for printing.
A city and comune in Friuli-Venezia Giulia autonomous region, Italy.
The capital and largest city of Bashkortostan, Russia.

(anatomy) The bone of the forearm that extends from the elbow to the wrist on the side opposite to the thumb, corresponding to the fibula of the hind limb. Also, the corresponding bone in the forelimb of any vertebrate.
Japanese apricot, a species of Asian plum, Prunus mume.
Not clouded.
Not pleasing; disagreeable.
So many as to be incapable of being counted.
(mathematics) Incapable of being put into one-to-one correspondence with the natural numbers or any subset thereof.
(grammar, of a noun) That cannot be used freely with numbers or the indefinite article, and therefore usually takes no plural form. Example: information.
(UK, dialectal) A light meal, particularly in the afternoon. [from 17th c.]
Not imprisoned.
ignorant, uneducated, untaught, untrained.
Unimpressive or unremarkable; dull and ordinary; nondescript.
Not ripe.
Developing too early; premature.
Not sprinkled with seasoning.
Not self-conscious; natural and genuine.
Not selfish.
The condition or state of being unusual.
Any of a set of authorless Hindu religious and philosophical texts considered to be an early source of the religion, found mostly as the concluding part of the Brahmanas and in the Aranyakas.
The process or result of growing up; progress; development.
A plumbing fixture intended for standing urination, typically by men and boys.
Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of bears.
A person who loans money to others and charges interest, particularly at an exorbitant, exploitative, or illegal rate.
Physical emptiness, an absence of matter; vacuum.
A word or phrase said upon leaving, such as "farewell" or "adieu".
A city in Castile and León, Spain.
The capital city of Malta.
A type of parchment paper made from the skin of a lamb, baby goat, or calf.
A writing paper of very high quality.
Able to be forgiven; worthy of forgiveness.
The art of projecting one's voice without moving the lips so that it appears to come from another source, such as a dummy.
72 BCE–46 BCE, the chieftain of the Arverni, leader of the great Gallic revolt against the Romans in 53–52 BCE.
A very acidic juice made by pressing unripe grapes.
Long, slender pasta, similar to spaghetti, only thinner.
A native or inhabitant of the city or province of Verona in the Veneto region in northern Italy.
Any of several leguminous plants, of the genus Vicia, often grown as green manure and for their edible seeds.
Any of many shrubs and trees, of the genus Viburnum, native to the Northern Hemisphere that have showy clusters of flowers.
A city and associated province of Veneto, Italy.
A department of Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. Capital and largest city: Poitiers.
A town and commune of Isère department, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.
A left tributary of the Loire in southwest France, flowing through the departments of Corrèze, Creuse, Haute-Vienne, Charente, Vienne and Indre-et-Loire.
A sauce, made of an acidic liquid such as vinegar or lemon juice; oil; and other ingredients, used as a salad dressing, or as a marinade for cold meats.
One who visits; a guest; a visitor.
The doctrine that life involves some immaterial "vital force", and cannot be explained scientifically.
A place artificially arranged for keeping or raising living animals.
(grammar) Used in address; appellative; said of the case or form of the noun, pronoun, or adjective by which a person or thing is addressed.
(historical) A geographic region of Eastern Europe, now divided between southeastern Poland, northwestern Ukraine, and southwestern Belarus.
A city, the administrative center of Voronezh Oblast, Russia.
(mineral) A rare mineral, copper telluride.
Pertaining to a fox.
Any of various small passerine birds, principally of genus Motacilla, of the Old World, notable for their long tails.
A band of fabric encircling the waist, especially a part of a pair of pants or a skirt.
(chiefly, British) A sleeveless, collarless garment worn over a shirt and under a suit jacket.
The state of being wakeful.
Of, from or relating to Wallonia.
A Romance language traditionally spoken in parts of southern Belgium and a small strip of northern France (around Givet).
A person who manages, or works in, a warehouse.
A sink in a bathroom, connected to a supply of water and a drain, in which one may wash one's face and hands.
A basin for washing the face and hands.
A domestic outbuilding used as a laundry.
(New Zealand) A room in a house used for washing laundry; a utility room.
A water-soluble pigment or paint.
A painting made by using such pigment.
This kind of painting as a genre.
Any channel, either natural or artificial, through which water flows.
A mill powered by water.
A whirlwind that forms over water, not associated with a mesocyclone of a thunderstorm (contrary to a true tornado).
A channel through which water is discharged, especially from the gutters of a roof.
A traveller, especially one on foot.
A weather vane, sometimes in the form of a cockerel.
(figuratively) One who veers with every change of current opinion; a fickle, inconstant person.
(uncountable, informal) Force on a pedal or increase to any fuel or power for an engine or motor.
A man who is a native or inhabitant of Wales.
A young offspring of a various carnivores (canid, ursid, felid, pinniped), especially of a dog or a wolf, the young of a bear or similar mammal (lion, tiger, seal); a pup, wolf cub.
(ambitransitive, of she-dog, she-wolf, vixen, etc.) To give birth.
(nautical) A bar, going through the poopdeck, attached perpendicularly to the tiller for steering a ship.
Crataegus monogyna, a hawthorn species native to Europe, northwest Africa and western Asia.
The weekend which includes Whitsunday.
The state or period of being a widower.
A surname from Korean.
Stubborn and determined.
A curve or bend.
Devoid of wind; calm.
The trachea.
(ambitransitive) To marry (a woman).
The Germanic chief god, distributor of talents and god of wisdom and war (corresponding to Odin), especially in his Anglo-Saxon form.
Considered typical of, stereotypical of, or appropriate to women; feminine.

Any of the terrestrial isopod crustaceans of suborder Oniscidea, which have a rigid, segmented exoskeleton, often being capable of rolling into a ball, and feed only on dead plant matter, usually living in damp, dark places, such as under stones or bark.
A ghost or specter, especially a person's likeness seen just after their death.
The larva of a mosquito.
A band that supports a wristwatch.
The capital city of the Sakha Republic (Yakutia), Russia
A residence of an official of the Chinese Empire.
A veil worn by Muslim women to cover parts of the face when they are in public.
(Norse mythology) An immense ash tree that is central in Norse cosmology, believed to connect the nine worlds.
A province in southwestern China, bordering Myanmar (Burma), Laos and Vietnam. Capital: Kunming.

A river in Southern Africa, flowing south and west from Zambia through east Angola and Zambia and then east along the border between Zambia and Zimbabwe into and through central Mozambique to the Indian Ocean.
A book of the Old Testament and the Hebrew Tanakh.
A town and rural district of Saxony, Germany.

replacements.english: (transitive) To regard (someone or something) as horrifying or detestable; to feel great repugnance toward. [First attested from around (1350 to 1470).]

(transitive, law) To annul (as a law, decree, ordinance, etc.) by an authoritative act; to abolish by the authority of the maker or their successor; to repeal.[First attested in the early 16th century.]
A state of being away or withdrawn from a place or from companionship
Failure to be present where one is expected, wanted, or needed; nonattendance; deficiency. [First attested around 1350 to 1470.]
Inattention to things present; abstraction (of mind). [First attested in the early 18th century.]
Lack; deficiency; non-existence. [First attested around 1350 to 1470.]
(political science, sociology) The principles or practice of absolute or arbitrary government; Synonym of despotism. [First attested in the early 19th century.]

(transitive) To include so that it no longer has separate existence; to overwhelm; to cause to disappear as if by swallowing up; to incorporate; to assimilate; to take in and use up. [first attested c. 1350 to 1470.]
(transitive) To suck up; to drink in; to imbibe, like a sponge or as the lacteals of the body; to chemically take in. [first attested in the early 17th century.]
Entire engrossment or occupation of the mind. [First attested in the mid 19th century.]
The act or process of absorbing or of being absorbed as,

Contrary to reason or propriety; obviously and flatly opposed to manifest truth; inconsistent with the plain dictates of common sense; logically contradictory; nonsensical; ridiculous; silly. [from mid-16th c.]
(now, chiefly, US) A member (especially a senior one) of the faculty at a college or university; an academic. [from 17th c.]
A member or follower of an academy, or society for promoting science, art, or literature, such as the French Academy, or the Royal Academy of Arts. [from 17th c.]
(transitive) To execute fully; to fulfill; to complete successfully.
Having many accomplishments, often as a result of study or training.
Something accomplished; an achievement.
The act of accomplishing; completion; fulfilment.
The state of being accurate; being free from error; exactness; correctness
Telling the truth or giving a true result; exact; not defective or faulty
A reward or other expression or token of gratitude.
An expression of gratitude for a benefit or an obligation.
The act of acknowledging
The act of recognizing in a particular character or relationship; recognition of existence, authority, truth, or genuineness.
(modal) In act or in fact; really; in truth; positively.
(transitive) To incite to action; to motivate.
Well skilled; completely versed; thoroughly proficient
(military) A high rank in the British and American Navies, NATO grade OF-9, equivalent ranks in other navies, in coast guards, etc.
(military) The appointed commander of a navy, regardless of formal title. [1440]
Fully grown.
An animal that is full-grown.
An opponent or rival.
(marketing) A commercial solicitation designed to sell some commodity, service or similar.
Concerned with beauty, artistic effect, or appearance.
That which appeals to the senses.
That which happens after, that which follows, usually of strongly negative connotation in most contexts, implying a preceding catastrophe.
The fear of wide open spaces, crowds, or uncontrolled social conditions.
Pleasant to the senses or the mind; pleasing, satisfying, palatable.
A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift.
A fine-grained white or lightly-tinted variety of gypsum, used ornamentally.
Depending on the throw of a die; random, arising by chance.

(uncountable, rhetoric) The use of symbols which may be interpreted to reveal a hidden, broader message, usually a moral or political one, about real-world issues and occurrences; also, the interpretation of such symbols.
(intransitive) To refer to something indirectly or by suggestion; to invoke it by implication rather than mention.
(transitive) To entice; to attract.
Having the power to allure.
(in the plural) The resins of the tree Aquilaria malaccensis (syn. Aquilaria agallocha), known for their fragrant aroma, produced after infection by the fungus Phialophora parasitica.
Any plant of the large and variable genus Aloe.

A writing system in which letters represent phonemes. (Contrast e.g. logography, a writing system in which each character represents a word, and syllabary, in which each character represents a syllable.)
The set of letters used when writing in a language.
A table or similar flat-topped structure used for religious rites.
(ambitransitive) To change the form or structure of.
(mathematics) Designating the members in a series, which regularly intervene between the members of another series, as the odd or even numbers of the numerals; every other; every second.
Happening by turns; one following the other in succession of time or place; first one and then the other (repeatedly).
Completely, wholly, or without exception.
A light, silvery metal extracted from bauxite, and a chemical element (symbol Al) with an atomic number of 13.
(inorganic chemistry) Any ionic derivative of ammonia in which a hydrogen atom has been replaced with a metal cation (R-NH- or R2N-)

(organic chemistry) Any derivative of an oxoacid in which the hydroxyl group has been replaced with an amino or substituted amino group; especially such derivatives of a carboxylic acid, the carboxamides or acid amides
(pathology) Loss of memory; forgetfulness.

An open, outdoor theatre (which may be a theatre in the round, or have a stage with seating on only one side), especially one from the classical period of ancient Greece or Rome, or a modern venue of similar design.
(chiefly, _, historical) A large vessel, especially a thin-necked clay vat used in ancient Greece and Rome for storing and transporting wine and oil.
(countable, analytical chemistry, physical chemistry) The process of breaking down a substance into its constituent parts, or the result of this process.

(uncountable, mathematics) A broad field of study in modern mathematics (often mentioned alongside algebra) which developed out of the calculus, concerned with the behavior of functions, sequences, series, limits, metric spaces, measures and more.
Decomposition into components in order to study (a complex thing, concept, theory, etc.).
Any small saltwater fish of the Engraulidae family, consisting of 160 species in sixteen genera, those of the genus Engraulis being widely sold as food.
(meteorology) An instrument for measuring and recording the speed of the wind, a windmeter.
Pertaining to, or having the form of, a ring; ring-shaped; in the shape of an annulus.

(astronomy) A ring of light in a celestial body, especially when caused by an annular eclipse (for example, when the Sun and Moon are in line with the Earth, but the Moon does not completely cover the Sun's disc).

(botany) A structure surrounding a sporangium (or part of it) which shrinks and causes it to rupture for spore dispersal; specifically, in a fern: a structure around about two-thirds of the sporangium consisting of differentially thick-walled cells which dry and distort the sporangium; and in a moss: a complete ring of cells around the tip of the sporangium which dissolves to cause the tip to detach.
(geometry) The region in a plane between two concentric circles of different radii.
(topology) Any topological space homeomorphic to the region in a plane between two concentric circles of different radii.
A ring- or donut-shaped area, object, or structure.
Against, opposed to.
(pharmaceutical drug) Any substance that can destroy or inhibit the growth of bacteria and similar microorganisms, generally transported by the lymphatic system.
(immunology) A protein produced by B-lymphocytes that binds to a specific antigen.
Synonym of antonymic.
Self-confidence; poise; composure.
(by extension) Of dubious veracity; of questionable accuracy or truthfulness; anecdotal or in the nature of an urban legend.
(computing) A computer program or the set of software that the end user perceives as a single entity as a tool for a well-defined purpose. (Also called: application program; application software.)
A verbal or written request for assistance or employment or admission to a school, course or similar.
Suitable or fit; proper; felicitous.
Relating to water; living in or near water, taking place in water.
(usually, of a decision) Based on individual discretion or judgment; not based on any objective distinction, perhaps even made at random.
Determined by impulse rather than reason; often connoting heavy-handedness.
(architecture) An architectural element having the shape of an arch
An arch-shaped arrangement of trapezoidal stones, designed to redistribute downward force outward.
An inverted U shape.
An archaic word, style, etc.
Difficult or exhausting to traverse.
Needing or using up much energy; testing powers of endurance.
(rare) An accepted (but deprecated and rarely used) metric unit of area equal to 100 square metres, or a former unit of approximately the same extent. Symbol: a.
(dance) Ellipsis of Argentine tango.
A citizen or descendant abroad of Argentina.
The rotating part of an electric motor or dynamo, which mostly consists of coils of wire (the winding) around a metal core.
A skilled manual worker who uses tools and machinery in a particular craft.
The quality of experiencing little or no sexual attraction.
(amino acid) A nonessential amino acid, L-α-amino-succinic acid, aminobutanedioic acid, found in animal protein and in sugar beet etc (C4H7NO4).
(transitive) To remove a liquid or gas by means of suction.
A violent onset or attack with physical means, for example blows, weapons, etc.
(transitive) To attack, physically or figuratively; to assail.
Hard-working, diligent or regular (in attendance or work); industrious.
A group of persons associated for a common purpose; an organization; society.
The act of associating.
Something resembling or shaped like an asterisk symbol.

(pathology) A long-term respiratory condition, in which the airways may unexpectedly and suddenly narrow, often in response to an allergen, cold air, exercise, or emotional stress. Symptoms include wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, and coughing.
The quality of being astute.
(analysis) A straight line which a curve approaches arbitrarily closely as it goes to infinity. The limit of the curve; its tangent "at infinity".
A type of island consisting of a ribbon reef that nearly or entirely surrounds a lagoon and supports, in most cases, one to many islets on the reef platform.

An atmospheric phenomenon created by charged particles from the sun striking the upper atmosphere, creating coloured lights in the sky. It is usually named australis or borealis based on whether it is in the Southern or Northern Hemisphere respectively.
Of, from, or pertaining to Australia, the Australian people or Australian languages.
A person from the country of Australia or of Australian descent.
A reliable, definitive source of information on a subject.
Power or right to make or enforce rules, give orders, or impose obligation; or a position having such power or right.
(formal) The earliest inhabitant of an area; an indigenous person.
(grammar) Relating to an auxiliary verb.
(obsolete) The vanguard of an army or other force.
Any group of people who invent or promote new techniques or concepts, especially in the arts.
Opposition or repugnance of mind; fixed dislike often without any conscious reasoning.
Exceedingly great; usually applied intensively.
Very bad.
Sky blue; resembling the clear blue colour of the unclouded sky.
(countable, and, uncountable) The clear blue colour of the sky; also, a pigment or dye of this colour.
(poetic) The unclouded sky; the blue vault above.
(tincture) A blue colour on a coat of arms, represented in engraving by horizontal parallel lines.
Beam, crossbeam; squared timber; a tie beam of a house, stretching from wall to wall, especially when laid so as to form a loft, "the balks".
Of or relating to the Balkan Peninsula.
(dances) A classical form of dance.
A theatrical presentation of such dancing, usually with music, sometimes in the form of a story.
Of or pertaining to the Baltic region or the Baltic Sea.
(North America, UK, Ireland) In particular, the sweet, yellow fruit of the Cavendish banana cultivar, which may be eaten raw, as distinct from e.g. a plantain for cooking.
An elongated curved tropical fruit of a banana plant, which grows in bunches and has a creamy flesh and a smooth skin. [from 1597]
A piece of currency made of paper or polymer. [from 18th c.]
Naked, uncovered.
A person who prepares coffee in a coffee shop for customers.
(mineral) A hard mafic igneous rock of varied mineral content; volcanic in origin, which makes up much of the Earth's oceanic crust.
(chiefly, US, South Africa, Canada, Philippines, Australia, euphemism) A lavatory (area where one washes or bathes): a room containing a toilet and (typically but not necessarily) a bathtub.
(Turkish, _, units of measure) A unit of weight established in 1931 equal to 10 kg.
(military) A ceremonial staff of a field marshal or a similar high-ranking military office.
(music) The stick of a conductor in musical performances.
(sports) An object transferred by runners in a relay race.
(electrical, electronics, countable) A device used to power electric devices, consisting of one or more electrically connected electrochemical cells or (archaically) electrostatic cells.
The principal ore of aluminium; a clay-like mineral, being a mixture of hydrated oxides and hydroxides.
(engineering) A pin which plays in and out of holes made to receive it, and which thus serves to engage or disengage parts of the machinery.
(military) A blade mounted to the end of a long gun, originally with a handle inserted into the bore, now usually attached underbarrel.
Possessing beauty, impressing the eye; attractive. [from 1520s]
A glass, bottle, or can of any of the above beverages.
An alcoholic drink fermented from starch material, commonly barley malt; often with hops or some other substance (like gruit) to impart a bitter flavor.
A living creature.
The state or fact of existence, consciousness, or life, or something in such a state.
(legal) To give or leave by will; to give by testament.
To give; to offer; to commit.
To hand down; to transmit.
A hand-held device consisting of a series of lenses and prisms, used to magnify objects so that they can be better seen from a distance, and looked at through both eyes.
A beginning or start; a point of origin.
An instance of childbirth.
The process of childbearing; the beginning of life; the emergence of a human baby or other viviparous animal offspring from the mother's body into the environment.
(of humans, or, other animals) Sexually attracted to both same-gendered and different-gendered partners. (Compare pansexual.)
A person who is bisexual.

A sticky, black, highly viscous liquid or semi-solid form of petroleum, burning with a bright flame. It occurs as an abundant natural product in many places, as on the shores of the Dead and Caspian Seas. It is used in cements, in the construction of pavements, etc.; Mineral pitch.
A fruit-bearing shrub of the aggregate species Rubus fruticosus and some hybrids.
(nautical) The side of a ship.
A committee that manages the business of an organization, e.g., a board of directors.
A device (e.g., switchboard) containing electrical switches and other controls and designed to control lights, sound, telephone connections, etc.
A relatively long, wide and thin piece of any material, usually wood or similar, often for use in construction or furniture-making.
Regular meals in a place of lodging; the price paid for them.
Short for blackboard, whiteboard, chessboard, surfboard, circuit board, message board (on the Internet), bulletin board, etc.
A menacing, ghost-like monster in children's stories.
(computing, slang) Incorrect, useless, or broken.
Counterfeit or fake; not genuine.
The trunk or stem of a tree.
The rounded seed-bearing capsule of a cotton or flax plant.
A large cushion or pillow, usually cylindrical in shape.
(transitive, often, figurative) To brace, reinforce, secure, or support.

A (usually) metal fastener consisting of a cylindrical body that is threaded, with a larger head on one end. It can be inserted into an unthreaded hole up to the head, with a nut then threaded on the other end; a heavy machine screw.
A bar of wood or metal dropped in horizontal hooks on a door and adjoining wall or between the two sides of a double door, to prevent the door(s) from being forced open.
A lightning spark, i.e., a lightning bolt.
A sliding pin or bar in a lock or latch mechanism.
An iron to fasten the legs of a prisoner; a shackle; a fetter.
(intransitive) To flee, to depart, to accelerate away suddenly.
A great ape, Pan paniscus, from Africa south of the Congo river.
(accounting) A person responsible for keeping records or documents, such as of a business.
A boxlike room or enclosure just big enough to accommodate one standing person, such as a phone booth or polling booth.
A small stall for the display and sale of goods.
A branch of biology concerned with the scientific study of plants.
(slang) Brother (a comrade or friend; one who shares one’s ideals).
(slang) Brother (a male sibling).
(slang, derogatory, usually in compounds) Someone, usually male, who aggressively evangelizes a person, concept or technology.
A house of prostitution.
A counter or sideboard from which food and drinks are served or may be bought.
A short printed publication, especially one produced regularly by an organization.
A time-sensitive news item or short news report.
A thatched or tiled one-story house in India surrounded by a wide veranda; a similar house in this style.
(chiefly, British) A desk, usually with a cover and compartments that are located above the level of the writing surface rather than underneath, and often used for storing papers.
An administrative unit of government; office.
An office (room where clerical or professional duties are performed).
Of a deep purple red color like that of Burgundy wine.
A tree, Theobroma cacao, whose seed is used to make chocolate.
The seed of this tree, the cocoa bean.
(literary, medicine) A dead body; especially the corpse of a human to be dissected. [from late 14th c.]
(chiefly, in, _, communism) The core of a managing group, or a member of such a group.
(military) The framework or skeleton upon which a new regiment is to be formed; the officers of a regiment forming the staff.
A frame or framework.
(vulcanology) A large crater formed by collapse of the cone or edifice of a volcano.

(weaponry) A metal object, usually small, with spikes arranged so that, when thrown onto the ground, one always faces up as a threat to pedestrians, horses, and vehicles (also used as a heraldic charge).
Close friendship in a group of friends or teammates.
(computer graphics, video games) The viewpoint in a three-dimensional game or simulation.
(photography) A device for taking still or moving pictures or photographs.
A darkened chamber in which the image of an outside object is projected and focused onto a surface.
(figurative) A person who is thought likely or worthy to gain a position or privilege. [from 1760s]

Without humor or expression of happiness; grave in manner or disposition

The cardinal number occurring after six hundred ninety-nine and before seven hundred one, represented in Arabic numerals as 700.
A pipe or channel, or system of pipes or channels, used to remove human waste and to provide drainage.

Arising from the fact of being male or female; pertaining to sex or gender, or to the social relations between the sexes. [from 17th c.]

A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.

A Swedish-style buffet comprising a variety of cold sandwiches and other dishes; (by extension) any buffet with a wide selection of dishes.

A social science that studies society, human social interaction, patterns of social relationships, and the interactions of culture. Through both theory and applied research, it engages subject matters across a range of microanalysis, mesoanalysis, and macroanalysis.
(furniture) An upholstered seat with a raised back and one or two raised ends, long enough to comfortably accommodate two or more people.

A member of a ground-based army, of any rank, but especially an enlisted member.
In English Canada and in parts of Australia, a type of lawyer who historically held the same role as above, but whose role has in modern times been merged with that of a barrister.

In many common law jurisdictions, a type of lawyer whose traditional role is to offer legal services to clients apart from acting as their advocate in court. A solicitor instructs barristers to act as an advocate for their client in court, although rights of audience for solicitors vary according to jurisdiction.
A bond of unity or agreement between individuals, united around a common goal or against a common enemy, such as the unifying principle that defines the labor movement; mutual support within a group.
Willingness to support another person in a difficult position or needs affection.
One of the two points in the ecliptic at which the sun is furthest from the celestial equator. This corresponds to one of two days in the year when the day is either longest or shortest.
Starting on one's feet, an instance of rotating one's body 360 degrees while airborne or on the ground, with one's feet passing over one's head.
At some unspecified point.
In an uncertain or unspecified location.
To an uncertain or unspecified location.
Capable of giving out a deep, resonant sound.
exhibiting sorrow; dejected; distraught; sad. (of a person)

(historical) A workers' council, an institution first formed during the 1905 Russian Revolution and then instituted as the main form of communist government at all levels in the Soviet Union; by extension, a similar organization in early Chinese communism and elsewhere.
A particular edible plant, Spinacia oleracea, or its leaves.
(architectural element) A tapering structure built on a roof or tower, especially as one of the central architectural features of a church or cathedral roof. [from 16th c.]
The top, or uppermost point, of anything; the summit. [from 17th c.]
Of or pertaining to the spirit or the soul.
(nautical) A long-handled tool used on ships for swabbing the decks and spreading protective coatings. [1844]
A vertical pole, post, or support.
(transitive) To make a stereotype of someone or something, or characterize someone by a stereotype.
(intransitive, figuratively) To suffer under uncomfortably hot conditions.
A floor or level of a building or ship.
(ecology) A layer of vegetation, usually of similar height.
(geology) A layer of sedimentary rock having approximately the same composition throughout.
A class of society composed of people with similar social, cultural, or economic status.
Any of the regions of the atmosphere, such as the stratosphere, that occur as layers.
One of several parallel horizontal layers of material arranged one on top of another.
(organic compound) A very toxic, colorless crystalline alkaloid, derived from nux vomica, used as a pesticide.
A place where radio or television programs, records or films are made.
An artist’s or photographer’s workshop or the room in which an artist works.

A systematic attempt to overthrow a government by working from within; undermining.
The act of overthrowing a government or a ruler; dethronement.
(carbohydrate) A disaccharide with formula C12H22O11, consisting of two simple sugars, glucose and fructose; normal culinary sugar.

(games) A type of pencil puzzle played on a square grid, requiring each of the columns, each of the rows, and each of the subregions (called "boxes" or "regions") to contain the lowest whole numbers up to the dimension of the puzzle, usually 1 to 9, once each. The subregions are rectangular or square except in variants.

(of a voluntarily-chosen course of action) Highly likely to certain to result in the deaths of the participants; dangerous or reckless to such a degree as to be tantamount to suicide for those taking part.
One who pursues someone, especially a woman, for a romantic relationship or marriage; a wooer; one who falls in love with or courts someone.
In excess of what is required or sufficient.
(figuratively) Exceptionally good; of the highest quality.

(grammar) The degree of comparison of an adjective or adverb used when comparing three or more entities in terms of a certain property or a certain way of doing something. In English, the superlative of superiority is formed by adding the suffix -est or the word most (e.g. tiniest, most fully); the superlative of inferiority, by adding the word least (e.g. least big, least fully).
(chiefly, philosophy) An imagined superior type of human being representing a new stage of human development; an übermensch, an overman. [from 1903.]
A man of extraordinary or seemingly superhuman powers.
Exalted, exquisite, superlative.
A belief or beliefs, not based on human reason or scientific knowledge, that events may be influenced by one's behavior in some magical or mystical way.
Any of the 114 chapters of the Quran.
(art) An artistic movement and an aesthetic philosophy that aims for the liberation of the mind by emphasizing the critical and imaginative powers of the subconscious.
A particular view; an examination, especially an official examination, of a particular group of items, in order to ascertain the condition, quantity, or quality.
An examination of the opinions of a group of people.
The act of surveying; a general view.
The fact or act of surviving; continued existence or life.
(intransitive) Of a person, to continue to live; to remain alive.
(intransitive) Of an object or concept, to continue to exist.
(transitive) To live longer than (someone); to outlive (someone or something); to outlast (something).
(transitive) To live past (a life-threatening event)
Initialism of sport utility vehicle; also S.U.V.

A cross with arms of equal length all bent halfway along at a 90° angle to the right or to the left, used as a religious symbol by various ancient and modern civilizations, but now mainly seen and used in the West (with arms angled to the right) as a symbol of Nazism and fascism.
A long bladed weapon with a grip and typically a pommel and crossguard (together forming a hilt), which is designed to cut, stab, slash and/or hack.
(ecology) Of, or relating to symbiosis; living together.
Of a relationship with mutual benefit between two individuals, organisms, or groups.
A device consisting of a hypodermic needle, a chamber for containing liquids, and a piston for applying pressure (to inject) or reducing pressure (to draw); a hypodermic syringe.
Excluded or forbidden from use, approach or mention.
An inhibition or ban that results from social custom or emotional aversion.
A vessel for brewing and serving tea, typically with a handle and spout.
A drop of clear, salty liquid produced from the eyes by crying or irritation.
Accompanied by tears; crying, or about to cry.

(chiefly, _, historical) The electrical device gradually developed in the early 19th century to transmit messages (telegrams) using Morse code; the entire system used to transmit its messages including overhead lines and transoceanic cables.
Effrontery; impudence.
(mathematics, obsolete) A norm operation on the quaternion algebra.
A partially enclosed glass container for displaying plants, especially plants that need high humidity.
An enclosure wherein small animals are displayed, usually with some plants, in a naturalistic setting.
Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome
(organic compound) The psychoactive substance present in cannabis, a hydrogenated derivative of cannabinol.
A member of any Germanic-language-speaking people, especially a German.
(arts) The quality given to a work of art by the composition and interaction of its parts.
(computer graphics) An image applied to a polygon to create the appearance of a surface.
The feel or shape of a surface or substance; the smoothness, roughness, softness, etc. of something.
A place or building, consisting of a stage and seating, in which an audience gathers to watch plays, musical performances, public ceremonies, and so on.
A region where a particular action takes place; a specific field of action, usually with reference to war.
Drama or performance as a profession or art form.
That or those belonging to them; the possessive case of they, used without a following noun.

(by extension) A lengthy essay written to establish the validity of a thesis (sense 1.1), especially one submitted in order to complete the requirements for a non-doctoral degree in the US and a doctoral degree in the UK; a dissertation.
(rhetoric) A proposition or statement supported by arguments.
In the third place; third in a row.
The cardinal number occurring after twelve and before fourteen: ten and three.
(chiefly, literary, or, law, archaic) To that place.
Marked by or showing lack of due thought or care.
(engineering) The quantitative point at which an action is triggered, especially a lower limit.
(figurative) The outset of something; the point of entry, or the beginning of an action.
The wage or salary at which income tax becomes due.
A row or range, especially one at a higher or lower level than another.
(card games) A sequence of three playing cards of the same suit. Tierce of ace, king and queen is called tierce-major.
(now, historical) A cask of a certain size, or its volume used as a measure of capacity. Larger than a barrel but smaller than a hogshead.
The quality of a sound independent of its pitch and volume.
(sports) A short break in the action of a sport, for substitution, consultation, etc.
(heraldry) A hue or pattern used in the depiction of a coat of arms.
(biology) A group of cells (along with their extracellular matrix if any) that are similar in origin and function together to do a specific job.
Thin, woven, gauze-like fabric.
Of or pertaining to Tocharian or the Tocharians.
At the same time, in the same place; in close association or proximity.

Any of various units of mass, originally notionally equal to the contents of a tun, particularly:

A series of games; either the same game played many times, or a succession of games related by a single theme; played competitively to determine a single winning team or individual.
(intransitive) To undergo a transformation; to change in appearance or character.

(electrical engineering) A static device that transfers electric energy from one circuit to another by magnetic coupling; primarily used to transfer energy between different voltage levels, which allows the most appropriate voltages for power generation, transmission and distribution to be chosen separately.
Passing or disappearing with time; transitory.
Remaining for only a brief time.
(astronomy) The passage of a celestial body or other object across the observer's meridian, or across the disk of a larger celestial body.
A surveying instrument rather like a theodolite that measures horizontal and vertical angles.
The act of passing over, across, or through something.
The conveyance of people or goods from one place to another, especially on a public transportation system; the vehicles used for such conveyance.
Lasting only a short time; temporary.
(inexact, sometimes, _, proscribed) Synonym of interpreter, a person or thing that immediately interprets direct speech. [1850]
A person or thing that translates meaning from one language into another, particularly
(medicine) A transplanted organ or tissue: a graft.
(medicine) An operation (procedure) in which tissue or an organ is transplanted: an instance of transplantation.
(historical) A deported convict.
A vehicle used to transport (passengers, mail, freight, troops etc.)
An act of transporting; conveyance.
One who travels, especially to distant lands.
(archaic) sin
(legal) An intentional interference with another's property or person.
(legal) An assembly including one or more judges to conduct judicial business; a court of law.
(by extension) Anything that lives underground.
A member of a supposed prehistoric race that lived in caves or holes, a caveman.

A musical instrument in the brass family, having a cylindrical bore, and usually a sliding tube (but sometimes piston valves, and rarely both). Most often refers to the tenor trombone, which is the most common type of trombone and has a fundamental tone of B♭ˌ (contra B♭).
An object, usually in the form of a statuette, cup, or shield, awarded for success in a competition or to mark a special achievement.
(dated) Pertaining to, involving, or of the nature of a trope or tropes; figurative, metaphorical.
From, or similar to, a hot, humid climate.
(historical) An emperor of Russia (1547 to 1917) and of some South Slavic states.
(Ireland, United Kingdom, ) The training or instruction provided by a teacher or tutor.
(North American, Philippines) A sum of money paid for instruction (such as in a private school, boarding school, university, or college).
(oncology, pathology) An abnormal growth; differential diagnosis includes abscess, metaplasia, and neoplasia.
Confused, agitated noise as made by a crowd.
A device, electronic or mechanical, that helps a person tune a musical instrument by showing the deviation of the played pitch from the desired pitch.
A person who tunes a piano or organ.
Any of various rotary machines that use the kinetic energy of a continuous stream of fluid (a liquid or a gas) to turn a shaft.
(botany) An Indian plant (Curcuma longa), with aromatic rhizomes, part of the ginger family (Zingiberaceae).
(cooking) The pulverized rhizome of the turmeric plant, used for flavoring and to add a bright yellow color to food.
A yellow to reddish-brown dye extracted from the turmeric plant.
Having a pale greenish-blue colour.
(countable, and, uncountable) A pale greenish-blue colour, like that of the gemstone.
A sky-blue, greenish-blue, or greenish-gray semi-precious gemstone.
The cardinal number occurring after one hundred ninety-nine and before two hundred one, represented in Arabic numerals as 200.
Appearing to be everywhere at once; being or seeming to be in more than one location at the same time.
Being everywhere at once: omnipresent.
Initialism of unidentified flying object; (loosely) an alien spacecraft. [from 20th c.]
Indicating the last item.
Not comfortable; causing discomfort.
Not conscionable; unscrupulous and lacking principles or conscience.

Happening through bad luck, or because of some unfortunate event.
Involving the joint activity of multiple agents.
Joined into a single entity.
Oneness; the state or fact of being one undivided entity.
ignorant, uneducated, untaught, untrained.
Not needed or necessary.
Located on a higher floor or level of a building.
Up the stairs; on or to a higher floor or level.
Forming words with the sense of “proto-, primitive, original”.
The element with atomic number 92 and symbol U. A radioactive silvery-grey metal in the actinide series.
(transitive) To employ; to apply; to utilize.
That has or have previously been owned by someone else.
Used to describe a past habit or state that no longer exists.
Most of the time; less than always, but more than occasionally.
The most extreme; greatest, ultimate.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc. [from 15th c.]
A stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure. [from 19th c.]
An extended period of time away from work or school.
Freedom from some business or activity. [from 14th c.]
(uncountable, sociology) The value which a person places on something.
(US) A person employed to clean or park cars.
(professional wrestling) A female performer in professional wrestling, acting as either a manager or personal chaperone; often used to attract and titillate male members of the audience.
(statistics) The second central moment in probability.
Streaked, spotted, or otherwise marked with a variety of color.
An upright open container used mainly for displaying fresh, dried, or artificial flowers.
(figuratively) Anything resembling such a downward-facing concave structure, particularly the sky and caves.
(gymnastics) A gymnastic movement performed on this apparatus.
An arched masonry structure supporting and forming a ceiling, whether freestanding or forming part of a larger building.
Any cellar or underground storeroom.

(figuratively, derogatory) A person whose brain (or, infrequently, whose body) has been damaged to the point that they cannot interact with the surrounding environment; a person in a persistent vegetative state.
A plant raised for some edible part of it, such as the leaves, roots, fruit or flowers, but excluding any plant considered to be a fruit, grain, herb, or spice in the culinary sense.
The edible part of such a plant.
A person who does not eat animal flesh, or, in some cases, use any animal products. [from 1839]
A conveyance: a spacious device for carrying or transporting substances, objects, people or animals.
A medium for expression of talent or views.
A dry, or sweet apéritif wine flavored with aromatic herbs, and often used in mixed drinks.
(architecture) Of or related to local building materials and styles; not imported.
Of or pertaining to everyday language, as opposed to standard, literary, liturgical, or scientific idiom.
Everyday speech or dialect, including colloquialisms, as opposed to standard, literary, liturgical, or scientific idiom.
Language unique to a particular group of people.
The language of a people or a national language.
A specific form or variation of something.
Of or relating to the medical or surgical treatment of non-human animals, especially domestic and farm animals.
A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
Of or pertaining to Vietnam.
(countable, chiefly, in the plural) A person or people from Vietnam or of Vietnamese descent.
Vietnamese cuisine; traditional Vietnamese food.
A person who acts outside of legal authority, often violently, to punish or avenge a crime, right a perceived wrong, etc.
A grape plantation, especially one used in the production of wine.
For practical purposes, though not technically; almost complete, very near.
(historical) A small horse-drawn carriage for two people sitting facing each other.
A person holding a corresponding position in another organisation.
In relation to; compared with.
Opposite; across from; set so as to be facing.
Related to or affecting the vision.

A vent or fissure on the surface of a planet (usually in a mountainous form) with a magma chamber attached to the mantle of a planet or moon, periodically erupting forth lava and volcanic gases onto the surface.
(electricity) The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
(intransitive) To be moved, or to pass, on a buoyant medium; to float.
A large Arctic marine mammal related to seals and having long tusks, tough, wrinkled skin, and four flippers, Odobenus rosmarus.
A ballroom dance in 3/4 time.
A piece of music for this dance (or in triple time).
(linguistics) A loanword that has spread to many different languages, often through trade or the adoption of foreign cultural practices.
A place with no remaining resources; a desert.
Wet, soggy or soaked with water.
A special fondness or obsessing desire.

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of magnetic flux; the flux linking a circuit of one turn that produces an electromotive force of one volt when reduced uniformly to zero in one second. Symbol: Wb.
The fourth day of the week in many religious traditions, and the third day of the week in systems using the ISO 8601 norm; it follows Tuesday and precedes Thursday.
The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in Western countries, Saturday and Sunday.
Deviating from the normal; bizarre.
Having an unusually strange character or behaviour.
(mythology) A person who is transformed or can transform into a wolf or a wolflike human, often said to do so during a full moon.
(of a wind) Blowing from the west; westerly.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the west.

(Should we move, merge or split([http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wiktionary%3ARequests+for+moves%2C+mergers+and+splits&action=edit&section=new&preload=Template:rfm-sense/preload&preloadtitle=%5B%5Bnorthern+and+western%23rfm-sense-notice-en-%7cwestern%5D%5D +]) this sense?) (cinema) A film, or some other dramatic work, set in, the historic (c. 1850–1910) American West (west of the Mississippi river) focusing on conflict between whites and Indians, lawmen and outlaws, ranchers and farmers, or industry (railroads, mining) and agriculture.
Regardless of the ... that; for any ... that.
A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.
(fused relative) The place in, at or to which.
In a/the situation, position, case, etc. in which.
In what situation.
In, at or to the place (that) or a place (that).
The place in which something happens.
(mostly, law) It being the fact that; inasmuch as. (Often used to begin recitals; sometimes emboldened or emphasized as a signifier.)
In contrast; whilst on the contrary; although.
A clause, as in legal documents, stating whereas.

(disease) A contagious disease of the respiratory system that usually affects children caused by the bacterium Bordetella pertussis, the advanced stage of which is characterised by spasms of coughing followed by a whooping sound during the intake of breath.
(slang) Excellent; awesome; masterful.
Evil or mischievous by nature; morally reprehensible.
(banking, finance) To extract (money) from a bank account or other financial deposit.
To draw or pull (something) away or back from its original position or situation.
To remove (someone or (reflexive, archaic) oneself) from a position or situation; specifically (military) , to remove (soldiers) from a battle or position where they are stationed.
To take away or take back (something previously given or permitted); to remove, to retract.
(intransitive) To stay back, to refrain.
(transitive) To keep (a physical object that one has obtained) to oneself rather than giving it back to its owner.
(archaic, or, humorous) One's habitual way of doing things; custom, habit, practice.
Spanning the world; global.
(uncountable, formal) Great anger; (countable) an instance of this.
A German- or Austrian-style sausage.
The language of these people, a Bantu language related to Zulu.
On the day before today.
The day immediately before today; one day ago.
The individual or group spoken or written to.
(object pronoun) The person spoken to or written to, as an object. (Replacing thee; originally as a mark of respect.) [from 13th c.]
That or those belonging to you; the possessive second-person singular pronoun used without a following noun.
(currencies) The basic unit of money in China.
Of, or relating to, animals.
Of, or relating to, zoology.
The part of biology relating to the animal kingdom, including the structure, embryology, evolution, classification, habits, and distribution of all animals, both living and extinct.
The human sexual attraction or arousal to non-sapient animals.

batches.deferred_policy: Entries without explicit regional UK IPA in Wiktextract remain generated_fallback/deferred; ambiguous candidate lists are retained for audit but first explicit regional IPA is used as primary value.

batches.source: raw Wiktextract English data from user-downloaded raw-wiktextract-data.jsonl.gz
batches.source_license: Wiktionary/Wikimedia content under CC BY-SA/GFDL-compatible Wiktextract redistribution terms; preserve attribution to Wiktionary contributors
batches.selection_policy: All entries whose US IPA provenance was generated_fallback and whose exact normalized word + normalized part of speech had explicit Wiktextract General-American/US/American IPA. Existing UK IPA was not modified or inferred.

replacements.english: (neuroanatomy) Each of the sixth pair of cranial nerves that are the motor nerves supplying the lateral rectus in the eye. [19th century.]

(astronomy) A ring of light in a celestial body, especially when caused by an annular eclipse (for example, when the Sun and Moon are in line with the Earth, but the Moon does not completely cover the Sun's disc).

(botany) A structure surrounding a sporangium (or part of it) which shrinks and causes it to rupture for spore dispersal; specifically, in a fern: a structure around about two-thirds of the sporangium consisting of differentially thick-walled cells which dry and distort the sporangium; and in a moss: a complete ring of cells around the tip of the sporangium which dissolves to cause the tip to detach.
(geometry) The region in a plane between two concentric circles of different radii.
(topology) Any topological space homeomorphic to the region in a plane between two concentric circles of different radii.
A ring- or donut-shaped area, object, or structure.
(pathology, psychology) A condition where one does not possess a functioning "mind's eye" and cannot visualize imagery. [from 2015]
(mineral) A blue vitreous mineral; a basic hydrated copper carbonate, with the chemical formula Cu2+3(CO3)2(OH)2, used since antiquity as a blue pigment or ores to extract metallic copper.
(North America) An unmarried woman.
A darkened chamber in which the image of an outside object is projected and focused onto a surface.
(pharmaceutical drug) A 4-aminoquinoline drug used to treat and prevent malaria, having the chemical formula C18H26ClN3.
One who daydreams.
The state when something (for example, a custom or a law) is no longer observed nor practised; disuse, obsolescence; (countable) an instance of this.

(by extension) Originally (historical) , a soldier armed with a dragoon musket (sense 1.1) who fought both on foot and mounted on a horse; now, a cavalier or horse soldier from a regiment formerly armed with such muskets.
A variety of pigeon, originally a cross between a horseman and a tumbler.

A digital graphic icon with a unique code point used to represent a concept, object, person, animal or place, originally used in Japanese text messaging but since adopted internationally in other contexts such as social media. Or, by extension, any non-standard emoji-like image inserted inline in text, i.e. an image emoticon.
Abnormal redness and inflammation of the skin, due to vasodilation.
(by extension) A leader, especially one exercising the powers of a tyrant.
(metrology) An SI unit of length equal to 100 metres. Symbol: hm
(book word, not comparable) Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of goats.

(musical instrument) A stringed instrument that produces a droning sound by turning a handle that connects to a wheel that rubs against a rosined string, with a keyboard also used to alter the pitch of the string.
A picture or symbol which represents the idea of something without indicating the sequence of sounds used to pronounce it. Examples include digits, traffic signs, and graphic symbols such as @.

A Japanese syllabary used when writing words borrowed from foreign languages other than Chinese, specific names of plants and animals and other jargon, onomatopoeia, or to emphasize a word or phrase. Also used to write the Ainu language.
A person who offers unsolicited views, advice, or criticism; one who kibitzes.
(chiefly, mammalogy, also, figuratively) Of, relating to, or characteristic of a hare or a rabbit.
The audience that listens to a certain form or genre of audio material (specifically (Internet, radio) , an audio broadcast such as a radio program or a podcast).
A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
(physics, cosmology) The hypothetical group of all the possible universes in existence.
A pocket monster; a fictional creature from the universe of the Pokémon franchise.
(hydrology, oceanography) A naturally formed transient area of open water surrounded by sea ice, especially in polar or subpolar seas. [from mid 19th c.]
(loosely, proscribed) Any pterosaur.
A pterosaur of the genus Pterodactylus.
(physics, uncountable, uncommon) Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see pyroclastic,‎ flow: flow (uncountable) of pyroclastic particles.
A mixture which is racemic.
Of or pertaining to rivers, or located on or by a river; inhabiting a river or its surrounds.
(South Africa) Various shrubs of the genus Combretum.
The tisane (herbal tea) made from this plant.
Of, from, or pertaining to the region of Samogitia, or its people.

(phonology) Any of a wide variety of phonological processes that occur at morpheme or word boundaries, such as the fusion of sounds across word boundaries and the alteration of sounds due to neighboring sounds or due to the grammatical function of adjacent words.
A wide, brown variety of edible mushroom, Lentinula edodes.
(slang, vulgar) A toilet or lavatory; an outhouse.
(pejorative, slang) A lewd and disreputable person, often female, especially an unattractive person with an air of tawdry promiscuity.

(games) A type of pencil puzzle played on a square grid, requiring each of the columns, each of the rows, and each of the subregions (called "boxes" or "regions") to contain the lowest whole numbers up to the dimension of the puzzle, usually 1 to 9, once each. The subregions are rectangular or square except in variants.
